<solution>
<![CDATA[
package com.example.service;

import com.example.model.UserPreferences;
import flexjson.JSONDeserializer;
import flexjson.JSONException;

public class ConfigService {

    // Reusable JSONDeserializer instance (thread-safe once configured)
    private static final JSONDeserializer<UserPreferences> DESERIALIZER = new JSONDeserializer<UserPreferences>()
            .useFields(true); // Enable direct field access since UserPreferences has no setters

    /**
     * Implements dynamic configuration loading functionality for user preferences.
     * Accepts a JSON-formatted string and deserializes it into a UserPreferences object using the Flexjson library.
     *
     * @param jsonConfig JSON-formatted configuration string representing user preferences
     * @return The deserialized UserPreferences object
     * @throws IllegalArgumentException if jsonConfig is null, empty, or has invalid JSON format for UserPreferences
     */
    public UserPreferences loadConfig(String jsonConfig) {
        if (jsonConfig == null) {
            throw new IllegalArgumentException("JSON configuration string cannot be null.");
        }
        String trimmedConfig = jsonConfig.trim();
        if (trimmedConfig.isEmpty()) {
            throw new IllegalArgumentException("JSON configuration string cannot be empty.");
        }

        try {
            return DESERIALIZER.deserialize(trimmedConfig, UserPreferences.class);
        } catch (JSONException e) {
            throw new IllegalArgumentException("Invalid JSON format for user preferences: " + e.getMessage(), e);
        } catch (RuntimeException e) {
            throw new IllegalArgumentException("Unexpected error loading user preferences: " + e.getMessage(), e);
        }
    }
}
